competitor_patterns: Extreme word count: top-ranking pages like Bikerumor (24,489 words) and road.cc (9,666 words) are comprehensive buyer guides, not thin reviews — our 1,092–1,352 word posts are outgunned by 7–18x
Structured comparison format: top competitors use named H2s per product (e.g., 'Oakley Sutro Lite', 'POC Devour', 'Tifosi Rail') giving Google clear entity signals and users easy navigation

Tested credibility signals: Bikerumor leads with 'Bikerumor Tested' in the title and road.cc uses 'Here are the best we've tested' — real-world testing authority is explicitly signalled in title and snippet

Lens education as a pillar: road.cc dedicates H2s to 'Which lens is best?' and 'What to look for in a pair of cycling glasses' — informational depth around lens choice earns featured snippets and long dwell time

Evileye ranks #1 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' with a dedicated product-feature page — competitors own high-intent feature keywords by building standalone pages around single features rather than bundling everything
our_gaps: Word count deficit is critical: our posts average ~1,250 words vs competitors averaging 9,000–24,000 words on the same target keywords — Google is rewarding exhaustive guides, not short reviews

No standalone interchangeable lens page: Evileye owns position 1 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' and we have no dedicated page or post targeting this keyword despite it being a core StradaPro advantage

Missing per-product H2 structure: our posts use thematic H2s ('Why Most Glasses Fail') rather than named competitor H2s — this means Google cannot extract clear entity comparisons and users cannot scan for the brand they already know

Zero clicks on 'alternatives to oakley' despite 6,850 impressions at position 7.1 — our title/meta for this query is not compelling enough to earn a single click, suggesting a meta title mismatch with searcher intent
No tables in most posts: the post ranking best has a table, but the majority do not — competitors use comparison tables heavily for scannability and featured-snippet eligibility on 'best X' queries
keyword_opportunities: interchangeable lens cycling glasses — Evileye owns this; StradaPro's click-in VellutoPuro/VellutoVisione system is a direct answer, and we have no dedicated page
lightweight cycling sunglasses — 25g frame is our strongest hardware differentiator; no post targets this directly and it maps to a clear purchase-intent query
cycling sunglasses with anti-fog — anti-fog is a built-in StradaPro feature and a high-intent modifier that buying-guide competitors rarely build dedicated pages around
oakley sutro lite alternative — Bikerumor and road.cc both name the Sutro Lite as a top pick, making it the reference product; a direct comparison post targets users already in the decision stage
30 day trial cycling glasses — risk-free trial is a unique commercial differentiator with no search competition; capturing this query builds brand-safe transactional traffic with no competing bids
next_post_guidelines: Target minimum 3,000 words per post — for 'best cycling glasses 2026' cluster, aim for 5,000+ to be competitive with road.cc (9,666) and Bikerumor (24,489); use real ride-condition scenarios to add depth without padding

Structure every comparison post with one H2 per named competitor brand (Oakley Sutro Lite, POC Devour, Tifosi Rail, Julbo Density, Knockaround Campeones) followed by a Velluto StradaPro H2 — this mirrors exactly what top-ranking pages do and signals entity relevance to Google

Add a mandatory comparison table in every post: columns should include Weight, Lens System, Anti-Fog, UV Rating, Trial Period, Price — this directly targets featured-snippet eligibility and the StradaPro wins on weight, trial, and lens interchangeability

Lead every title and H1 with the year (2026) and the tested/reviewed signal: e.g. 'Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: Tested on 50+ Hours of Road Riding' — mirrors the credibility pattern that earns clicks from Bikerumor and road.cc titles

Dedicate one post immediately to 'Alternatives to Oakley Cycling Glasses 2026' — this query has 6,850 impressions and 0 clicks; the page needs a meta title with an explicit value hook ('7 Lighter, Cheaper Alternatives to Oakley — Tested 2026') and must open with the search intent acknowledged in the first paragraph

Include a 'Lens Guide' H2 in every eyewear post that explains tint categories, light conditions, and when to use clear vs high-contrast lenses — this mirrors road.cc's 'Which lens is best?' section which contributes to their 9,666-word depth and earns informational traffic alongside purchase traffic

Add a structured FAQ section (minimum 5 Q&As using HowTo or FAQ schema) to every post — our existing posts with FAQ already outperform those without; questions should mirror the exact GSC queries: 'Are there good alternatives to Oakley?', 'What are the best cycling glasses for 2026?', 'Do interchangeable lens glasses prevent fogging?'
seo_quick_wins: Add FAQ schema markup to all published posts — the posts already have FAQ sections but schema markup must be implemented in JSON-LD so Google can surface them as rich results, directly increasing CTR from current positions 7–9

Insert a comparison table (Weight / Lens System / Anti-Fog / Trial Period / Price) into every post that doesn't have one — this is the fastest way to qualify for featured snippets on 'best cycling glasses 2026' (pos 9.3) and push it into top 3

Standardise meta descriptions across all posts to include a specific numeric differentiator and CTA: e.g. 'Tested 2026: 25g frame, click-in lenses, 30-day trial. See how StradaPro compares to Oakley, POC & Tifosi →' — this pattern directly addresses the 0-click problem on high-impression queries
meta_title_fixes: QUERY: 'alternatives to oakley' (6,850 impressions, 0 clicks, pos 7.1) → New title: 7 Oakley Alternatives That Weigh Less & Cost Less (2026) | Why: 0 clicks from 6,850 impressions means the current title has zero differentiation; leading with a number, the direct query phrase, and two concrete benefits (weight + price) exploits the informational intent and creates a strong click hook at position 7

QUERY: 'veluto' (323 impressions, 0.3% CTR, pos 5.7) → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses — Official Shop | StradaPro 2026 | Why: This is a branded misspelling — the current title likely doesn't match the misspelled query or clarify we are the official source; including the correct brand name plus 'Official Shop' reassures searchers they've found the right destination and the year signals freshness

QUERY: 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' (142 impressions, 1.4% CTR, pos 9.3) → New title: Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: Tested & Ranked by Riders | Why: CTR of 1.4% at position 9 is below expected; adding 'Tested & Ranked by Riders' mirrors the Bikerumor credibility signal that dominates this SERP and differentiates from generic list titles
content_quick_wins: QUERY: 'alternatives to oakley' (pos 7.1, 6,850 impressions, 0 clicks) → Topic: '7 Best Alternatives to Oakley Cycling Sunglasses in 2026 — Lighter, Cheaper, Tested' | Add: Dedicated 3,500+ word page with one H2 per alternative brand, mandatory comparison table (weight/price/lens system/trial), explicit opening paragraph acknowledging the Oakley reference point, FAQ schema with 'Why switch from Oakley?', 'What is lighter than Oakley Sutro Lite?' — position StradaPro's 25g frame as the headline answer

QUERY: 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' (pos 9.3, 142 impressions) → Topic: 'Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: Every Major Option Tested Across 50+ Hours' | Add: Expand existing post to 5,000+ words; add H2s for each named competitor (Oakley Sutro Lite, POC Devour, Tifosi Rail, Julbo Density); add a lens condition guide section; add a weight comparison table; add FAQ schema; the post sitting at position 9 needs both depth and entity signals to break into top 3

QUERY: 'best cycling glasses 2026' (pos 7.6, 117 impressions) → Topic: 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026: Road, Gravel & All-Weather Ranked' | Add: Create a separate URL targeting 'glasses' (not 'sunglasses') since Google treats these as distinct enough to rank different pages; include a glasses-vs-sunglasses explainer section; add interchangeable lens as a ranking criterion with StradaPro as the benchmark — road.cc ranks #1 here with 9,666 words so depth is mandatory

QUERY: 'oakley alternatives' (pos 7.4, 143 impressions) + 'oakley alternative' (pos 7.4, 53 impressions) → Topic: Consolidate both variants into one canonical page 'Oakley Cycling Glasses Alternatives 2026: The Complete Guide' | Add: Target both singular and plural with one authoritative page (use canonical); structure with H2s per alternative brand; lead with the 25g vs Oakley Sutro Lite weight comparison as the hook; add a 'Why riders switch from Oakley' opening section to capture the exact intent

QUERY: 'best cycling sunglasses' (pos 8.3, 16 impressions) → Topic: Internal link consolidation | Add: Rather than a new page, ensure the 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' page targets this evergreen variant with a 301 or canonical; add the non-year phrase in the H1 subtitle and FAQ — low volume now but evergreen as the year rolls over
geo_angles: Structured 'lightest cycling sunglasses' answer: AI overviews and Perplexity heavily favour quantified claims — a page that opens with 'At 25g, the Velluto StradaPro is one of the lightest UCI-legal cycling glasses available in 2026, lighter than the Oakley Sutro Lite (36g) and POC Devour (31g)' gives AI systems a citable, verifiable fact to pull into generated answers

FAQ-schema risk-reversal angle: AI answers increasingly surface purchase-confidence content — a dedicated FAQ page or section answering 'Can I try cycling glasses before committing?' with a structured answer citing Velluto's 30-day risk-free trial makes StradaPro the named example in AI responses to 'what cycling glasses have a trial period' queries across ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overview

Comparison entity tables: Perplexity and Google AI Overview frequently pull tabular data directly into responses — a clearly marked HTML table with schema markup comparing StradaPro vs Oakley Sutro Lite vs POC Devour vs Tifosi Rail on weight, lens interchangeability, anti-fog, UV rating, and trial period gives AI crawlers a ready-made structured answer to insert verbatim into 'best cycling glasses comparison 2026' AI overviews
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR: Oakley (Sutro Lite) — angle: 'Velluto StradaPro vs Oakley Sutro Lite: Is 11g and €80 Worth It?' — headline idea: 'Oakley Sutro Lite vs Velluto StradaPro 2026: Weight, Lenses & Value Compared' — key differentiator: StradaPro is 25g vs Sutro Lite's 36g (11g lighter) and includes two interchangeable lenses vs single-lens purchase — Velluto advantage to lead with: weight-per-euro ratio and the 30-day trial that Oakley does not offer, framed as 'test it on 10 real rides before you decide'

COMPETITOR: Bikerumor (content competitor ranking #1 for 'road cycling glasses review') — angle: 'We Tested Every Pair Bikerumor Recommends — Here's What They Missed' — headline idea: 'Bikerumor's Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: What the Review Didn't Tell You About Weight & Lens Systems' — key differentiator: Bikerumor does not include Velluto in their roundup; a post that references their top picks (Julbo Density, Oakley Sutro Lite, POC Devour) and adds a 'what we'd add to this list' angle earns referential traffic from users who read Bikerumor first — Velluto advantage to lead with: Italian design, 25g frame, and click-in lens system not featured in their guide

COMPETITOR: Evileye (ranks #1 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses') — angle: 'Velluto StradaPro vs Evil Eye Cycling Glasses: Which Interchangeable Lens System Is Actually Faster to Swap?' — headline idea: 'Best Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses 2026: Velluto StradaPro vs Evil Eye — Tool-Free Swap Test' — key differentiator: VellutoPuro (clear) + VellutoVisione (high-contrast) dual-lens system with tool-free click-in vs Evil Eye's system — Velluto advantage to lead with: both lenses included in the purchase price with the 30-day trial, vs buying lenses separately with most competitors
